title: CapabilityManifest
description: Canonical manifest for a discrete, verifiable capability in VistA Evolved. Defines identity, lifecycle, readiness, scope, pack/adapter dependencies, claim posture, visibility posture, and activation semantics. Fulfills capability-truth-and-claim-gating-spec.md §19.2 (deferred schema) and pack-and-adapter-architecture-governance.md §19.1 (capability manifest structure). Instances live in packages/contracts/capability-manifests/ and are named {capabilityId}.json.

capabilityId.description: Unique capability identifier. Convention: {capabilityClass}.{qualifier} (e.g. 'billing.ph-philhealth-claims', 'clinical.language-support'). Must match the capabilityId referenced in pack-manifest capabilityContributions.

displayName.description: Human-readable name (e.g. 'PhilHealth Claims Billing', 'English Language Support').

description.description: What this capability means and what it enables when fully realized.

capabilityClass.description: Named grouping of related capabilities sharing a readiness domain. Per capability-truth spec §5.

version.description: Semver version of this capability manifest.

lifecycle.description: Governance and lifecycle metadata for this capability manifest.

readiness.description: Current governed readiness state per capability-truth spec §7. Readiness is scoped — the state applies within the declared scopeBounds.

scope.description: Where this capability is applicable. Defines legal-market, facility-type, service-line, and tenant-scope boundaries.

packDependencies.description: Packs that this capability requires or optionally benefits from. Per pack-and-adapter §19.1.

adapterDependencies.description: Adapters/connectors this capability requires or optionally benefits from. Per capability-truth spec §14.2.

claimPosture.description: Claim-gating posture: what can be truthfully stated about this capability on public surfaces. Per capability-truth spec §10, §11.

visibilityPosture.description: Control-plane and tenant-admin visibility posture. Supports future UI decisions without overbuilding that layer now. Per capability-truth spec §16.

activationSemantics.description: How this capability becomes active: automatic when packs are active, operator-selectable, or blocked pending dependencies. Per capability-truth spec §12.

notes.description: Free-form notes, constraints, or context. Must not contain readiness claims — use readiness block for those.

capabilityClassEnum.description: Capability class groupings per capability-truth spec §5. clinical = clinical workflows and content. billing = RCM/payer/claims. scheduling = appointment/resource. localization = language/locale. regulatory = compliance/regulatory. platform = platform-native infrastructure capabilities. imaging = radiology/DICOM. telehealth = virtual care. analytics = reporting/BI. interop = HL7/FHIR/integration.

readinessStateEnum.description: The 9-state readiness lifecycle from capability-truth spec §7.1 and country-payer-readiness-registry spec §10.1. Transitions: declared→specified→implemented→validated→verified→claimable→production-eligible; degradation possible; deprecated→retired.

lifecycleBlock.description: Governance metadata for the capability manifest itself.

state.description: Manifest document lifecycle state (not the capability readiness state). draft = initial authoring. active = accepted and governing. deprecated = being phased out. retired = no longer valid.

owner.description: Team or role responsible for this capability's truth (e.g. 'platform-team', 'ph-market-team').

readinessBlock.description: Governed readiness state for this capability. Per capability-truth spec §7. State is always scoped — it applies within the declared scopeBounds.

evidenceSummary.description: Brief summary of what evidence supports the current state. Must be truthful — no overclaiming. Reference artifacts/ paths if evidence exists.

scopeBounds.description: Explicit bounds within which the readiness state is valid. Per capability-truth spec §9.2 — truth is never flat, always scoped.

constrainingFactors.description: Factors that prevent the capability from advancing to a higher readiness state.

scopeBlock.description: Where this capability is applicable. Empty arrays mean no restriction on that dimension.

legalMarkets.description: Legal-market IDs (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) where this capability applies. Empty = global.

facilityTypes.description: Facility-type identifiers where this capability applies. Empty = any.

serviceLines.description: Service-line identifiers where this capability applies. Empty = any.

tenantScopes.description: Tenant-scope identifiers (e.g. 'single-tenant', 'multi-tenant') where this capability applies. Empty = any.

packDependencyEntry.description: A pack this capability depends on. Per pack-and-adapter §19.1.

packId.description: Pack ID from the pack manifest registry.

packFamily.description: Pack family for context (language, locale, regulatory, etc.).

relationship.description: required = capability is not achievable without this pack. enhances = capability works without this pack but gains features with it.

rationale.description: Why this pack is needed for this capability.

adapterDependencyEntry.description: An adapter this capability depends on. Per capability-truth spec §14.2.

adapterId.description: Adapter identifier matching adapterRequirements in pack manifests.

required.description: Whether the adapter is required (true) or optional/enhancing (false).

currentStatus.description: Current known status of this adapter. Must be truthful.

fallbackBehavior.description: What happens when the adapter is unavailable. Required adapters must specify 'integration-pending'.

claimPostureBlock.description: Claim-gating posture per capability-truth spec §10, §11. Governs what can be truthfully stated on public surfaces.

currentClaimLevel.description: no-claim = capability must not be mentioned publicly. internal-only = visible to internal team only. roadmap-disclosed = may appear in roadmaps labeled as planned/in-development. bounded-claim = may be claimed publicly within explicit scope bounds. general-claim = may be claimed publicly without scope qualifiers.

permittedScopeStatement.description: The maximum truthful public statement about this capability. Must not exceed what readiness state and evidence support.

prohibitedClaims.description: Specific claims that are explicitly prohibited despite current state. Per anti-drift rules in capability-truth spec §4.

visibilityPostureBlock.description: Visibility posture for control-plane and tenant-admin surfaces. Conceptual per capability-truth spec §16 — supports future UI decisions without building that layer now.

controlPlaneVisibility.description: hidden = not shown in control-plane. visible-disabled = shown but not activatable. visible-enabled = shown and activatable.

tenantAdminVisibility.description: hidden = not shown to tenant admins. visible-disabled = shown but not configurable. visible-enabled = shown and configurable.

rationale.description: Why this visibility posture was chosen.

activationSemanticsBlock.description: How this capability becomes active. Per capability-truth spec §12.

activationMode.description: automatic = capability activates when all required packs and adapters are active. operator-selectable = requires explicit operator activation even when dependencies met. dependency-blocked = cannot activate because required dependencies are not met.

activationConstraints.description: Constraints that must be satisfied before activation is possible.

deactivationConstraints.description: Constraints on deactivating this capability (e.g. other capabilities depend on it).
